content.title: This work integrates 6 K-BERDL databases + 1 external reference catalog across 10 notebooks — all within the `beril` tenant.

slides.speaker_notes_seed: RB-TnSeq is the primary fitness workhorse — 198 Caulobacter experiments across 3943 genes, grounding the phenotype-bearing rate calibration and the Fur-regulon enrichment test (Findings 1–2); PaperBLAST is the annotation backbone spanning DEG characterization through the comparative species presence/absence panel (Findings 3–6); GO terms (NB03) characterized ChvI phase themes, Pfam (NB05) anchored the PG-remodeling gene set, and PubMed + isolates supported literature anchoring and strain references. RefSeq (external, NCBI) confirmed the NB06b comparative absence calls that make Finding 6 robust against PaperBLAST's ~80% false-negative rate for Caulobacter lipid A genes. All 10 notebooks draw from the single `beril` tenant — this project did not span multiple BERDL tenants.
